text: Effectively managing a team requires a balance of several key skills. Clear communication is essential; you must share goals and expectations so everyone understands their role. Delegation is also important—assign tasks based on each member's strengths, and trust them to complete the work. Regular, constructive feedback helps team members improve and feel valued, while recognition boosts motivation. Finally, conflicts can arise, so address them calmly by listening to all sides and finding a fair solution. A good manager who practices these skills builds a productive, positive team environment where everyone can succeed.
